ddls:
CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation */,
  "code" TEXT /* Unique code for the occupation */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the industry to which the occupation belongs */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the occupation */,
  "average_salary" REAL /* Average salary for the occupation */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Projected growth rate of the occupation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level (e.g., less hs, high school, some college) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the education level */,
  "completion_rate" REAL /* Average completion rate for this education level */,
  "average_duration" INTEGER /* Average duration (in years) to complete this education level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_data" (
  "employment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment data entry */,
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the occupation */,
  "education_level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the education level */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the employment data */,
  "employment_number" REAL /* Number of people employed with the specified occupation and education level */,
  "median_salary" REAL /* Median salary for the occupation and education level */,
  "employment_rate" REAL /* Employment rate for the occupation and education level */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "data_source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_occupation_id FOREIGN KEY ("occupation_id") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_education_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_level_id") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, policymaker, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "employment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employment data being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_employment_id FOREIGN KEY ("employment_id") REFERENCES employment_data ("employment_id")
)
